cluster_fsm.spec_reference: Matter 1.5 Application Cluster Specification, Section 8.11, Pages 664-666

features.name: OnOff
features.description: Dependency with the OnOff cluster. DISALLOWED for Oven Mode cluster per Revision 2. SHALL NOT be supported.

states.name: Bake
states.description: Device is in baking mode for baking food items. At least one mode with this tag MUST exist in SupportedModes (mandatory cluster invariant).
invariants: EXISTS m IN SupportedModes WHERE (0x4000 IN m.ModeTags)
states.safety_implications: Heating element active at baking temperatures — burn risk if door opened unexpectedly; high energy consumption during operation

states.name: Convection
states.description: Device is in convection mode creating airflow within the cavity during cooking duration.
states.safety_implications: Fan and heating element both active — burn risk from circulated hot air; motor failure may overheat cavity

states.name: Grill
states.description: Device is in grill (broil) mode for grilling food items. Referred to as Broil in many regions.
states.safety_implications: HIGH: Upper heating element at maximum intensity — severe burn risk from radiant heat; fire risk with fatty foods or flammable materials inside

states.name: Roast
states.description: Device is in roast mode for roasting food items at high dry heat.
states.safety_implications: Heating element active at high temperatures — burn risk

states.name: Clean
states.description: Device is in self-cleaning mode to clean internal components of the appliance using extreme heat (pyrolytic cleaning).
states.safety_implications: CRITICAL: Pyrolytic cleaning uses extreme temperatures (typically >480°C). Unauthorized remote activation creates severe burn and fire hazard. Spec does not mandate door locking, elevated access, or physical presence before activating this mode.

states.name: Convection_Bake
states.description: Device is in convection bake mode creating airflow within the device during baking duration.
states.safety_implications: Fan and heating element active — burn risk from circulated hot air

states.name: Convection_Roast
states.description: Device is in convection roast mode creating airflow within the device during roasting duration.
states.safety_implications: Fan and heating element active at high roasting temperatures — burn risk

states.name: Warming
states.description: Device is in warming mode which begins warming the cavity to maintain food temperature.
states.safety_implications: Low-level heating active — burn risk reduced but prolonged contact with cavity surfaces still hazardous

states.name: Proofing
states.description: Device is in proofing mode creating a warm, humid environment for bread proofing.
states.safety_implications: Gentle heat active — low burn risk; condensation from steam/humidity may affect electronics if door opened during operation

states.name: Steam
states.description: Device is in steam cooking mode using steam heat.
states.safety_implications: Steam and heat active simultaneously — scalding risk when door opened during operation; requires water reservoir management

states.name: Bake_and_Warm
states.description: Compound mode combining Bake and Warming tags. Example from spec Section 8.11.8: 'Bake and Warm' mode with tags 0x4000 and 0x4007.
states.safety_implications: Heating element active in combined mode — exact thermal behavior is implementation-defined; burn risk

states.name: Convection_Cook_and_Clean
states.description: Compound mode combining Convection and Clean tags. Example from spec Section 8.11.8: 'Convection Cook and Clean' mode with tags 0x4001 and 0x4004.
states.safety_implications: CRITICAL: Combined extreme temperature (pyrolytic cleaning) with active fan airflow. Thermal hazard is compounded. Semantics are entirely implementation-defined. No spec-level safety constraints exist.

transitions.guard_condition: NewMode IN SupportedModes && 0x4000 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags && NOT (0x4007 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags)

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Bake mode (e.g., active cleaning cycle)

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.6.1 SupportedModes (Bake tag mandatory); Section 8.11.7.1.1 Bake Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode

transitions.guard_condition: NewMode IN SupportedModes && 0x4001 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags && NOT (0x4004 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags)

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Convection mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1.2 Convection Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Grill mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1.3 Grill Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Roast mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1.4 Roast Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode

transitions.guard_condition: NewMode IN SupportedModes && 0x4004 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags && NOT (0x4001 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags)

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Implementation prohibits entering/exiting Clean mode while a cycle is already active or in unsafe state

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1.5 Clean Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode — NOTE: Only Operate-level access required despite extreme-temperature hazard

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Convection Bake mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1.6 Convection Bake Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Convection Roast mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1.7 Convection Roast Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode

transitions.guard_condition: NewMode IN SupportedModes && 0x4007 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags && NOT (0x4000 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags)

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Warming mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1.8 Warming Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Proofing mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1.9 Proofing Tag; Mode Base ChangeToMode

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Steam mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.7.1 Mode Tags (Steam, 0x4009); Mode Base ChangeToMode

transitions.guard_condition: NewMode IN SupportedModes && 0x4000 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags && 0x4007 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Bake and Warm mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.8 Mode Examples (Bake and Warm); Mode Base ChangeToMode

transitions.guard_condition: NewMode IN SupportedModes && 0x4001 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags && 0x4004 IN SupportedModes[NewMode].ModeTags

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not exist in SupportedModes list
Current operational state prevents switching to Convection Cook and Clean mode

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.8 Mode Examples (Convection Cook and Clean); Mode Base ChangeToMode — NOTE: Only Operate-level access required despite compound extreme-temperature hazard

error_responses.description: Requested mode index does not correspond to any entry in the SupportedModes attribute list; CurrentMode remains unchanged

transitions.spec_reference: Section 8.11.6 Attributes; Mode Base ChangeToMode error path

functions.name: BakeTagConstraintSatisfied

parameters.name: SupportedModes
parameters.description: Current value of the SupportedModes attribute

functions.description: Checks that at least one ModeOptionStruct entry in SupportedModes contains the Bake mode tag (0x4000) in its ModeTags field. Returns true if the mandatory cluster invariant holds, false otherwise. This constraint is specified in Section 8.11.6.1.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Cluster-level invariant. Must be validated whenever SupportedModes is written. Guards all transitions: a cluster where BakeTagConstraintSatisfied() is false is non-conformant.

functions.name: ModeTagPresent

parameters.name: mode_index
parameters.description: Index into SupportedModes list (value of NewMode in ChangeToMode command)

parameters.name: tag_value
parameters.description: Mode tag value to check for membership in the mode's ModeTags field

functions.description: Checks whether the ModeOptionStruct at the given index in SupportedModes contains the specified tag_value in its ModeTags field list. Used to determine which FSM state a requested mode maps to.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Used in guard conditions for T001–T012 to discriminate target states based on the tags of the requested NewMode.

functions.name: ModeIndexValid

parameters.name: NewMode
parameters.description: Mode value from ChangeToMode request field

functions.description: Determines whether NewMode corresponds to a valid Mode field value in any entry of SupportedModes. Returns false if NewMode is not found, triggering T013 (UnsupportedMode response) and leaving CurrentMode unchanged.
functions.usage_in_fsm: T013 guard condition. Also the first check performed before evaluating any of T001–T012.

operate_commands: ChangeToMode (0x00) — all mode transitions including safety-critical Clean and Grill modes

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Pyrolytic self-cleaning operates at extreme temperatures (typically exceeding 480°C). Remote activation via ChangeToMode command using only Operate-level access can trigger the cleaning cycle without physical presence verification, creating severe burn and fire hazard.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: NONE — Section 8.11 does not restrict access level for Clean mode activation, does not require timed interaction, physical button confirmation, or any elevated privilege. Implementation-defined only.

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Upper grill/broil heating element operates at maximum intensity. Remote activation with food, grease, or flammable materials inside the oven creates fire risk and severe burn hazard from radiant heat.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: NONE — no access restriction or confirmation mechanism specified

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Compound mode combining extreme pyrolytic cleaning temperatures with active fan circulation. Physical hazard is compounded relative to Clean mode alone. Semantics are entirely implementation-defined.

Transitioning into Clean mode requires only Operate privilege with no timed interaction, no physical confirmation, and no restriction on source state. Any fabric or node with Operate access can remotely initiate a pyrolytic cleaning cycle.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: NONE — timed_required is false; no Manage or Administer access required; no INVALID_IN_MODE mandatory response defined in spec for Clean mode

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: Transitioning into Convection_Cook_and_Clean compound mode requires only Operate privilege. Hazard is at least equal to Clean mode with added airflow dynamics.

ModeTags field permits 1 to 8 tags per mode. No constraint prohibits combining hazardous tags (e.g., Grill 0x4002 + Clean 0x4004, or Steam 0x4009 + Grill 0x4002). Tag combinations have no defined semantic safety validation in the spec.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: Only informational mode examples provided in Section 8.11.8; no normative constraint on hazardous tag combinations

safety_critical_elements.safety_concern: No event is generated when CurrentMode changes. A monitoring fabric cannot reliably detect unauthorized mode changes to hazardous modes (Clean, Grill) without continuously polling CurrentMode.
safety_critical_elements.mitigation_in_spec: NONE — no mandatory ModeChange event defined in Oven Mode or Mode Base cluster

spec_gaps.description: ChangeToMode command (inherited from Mode Base) uses Operate access for ALL mode transitions, including safety-critical modes such as Clean (pyrolytic, >480°C) and Grill. No access level differentiation per mode tag is defined.
spec_gaps.security_impact: An attacker with Operate-level access (e.g., a compromised smart home app, rogue fabric node, or unauthorized guest) can remotely activate self-cleaning or grill mode without physical presence, creating burn and fire hazard.

spec_gaps.description: No event is defined for CurrentMode changes in Mode Base or Oven Mode. Unlike clusters such as Door Lock (LockOperationEvent) or Alarm (AlarmNotification), there is no mechanism to asynchronously notify observers when the oven switches modes.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Silent unauthorized mode activations (e.g., Clean or Grill) cannot be detected without polling. Safety monitoring systems cannot rely on push notification of hazardous state entry.

spec_gaps.description: The DEPONOFF feature is explicitly disallowed (conformance 'X'). However, the spec does not define the required behavior if a non-conformant device enables this feature. Consequences for OnMode and StartUpMode operation in that case are not specified.
spec_gaps.security_impact: A non-conformant or malicious implementation could enable DEPONOFF to trigger unintended automatic mode changes when the OnOff cluster state changes, potentially activating hazardous oven modes on power-on.

spec_gaps.description: StartUpMode and OnMode are disallowed (conformance 'X'). No normative startup mode is defined. The mode the oven enters on power-on or reset is entirely implementation-defined with no spec constraint.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Depending on how the implementation initializes CurrentMode (e.g., from non-volatile storage), a power cycle could result in the oven entering a hazardous mode (Clean, Grill) on startup without explicit user command.

spec_gaps.description: SupportedModes must contain at least one entry with Bake tag (0x4000), but no formal constraint defines which mode is selected as default/initial, which modes are optional, or what transition sequences are valid. The list is fully implementation-defined beyond the Bake tag requirement.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Implementations may diverge significantly in available modes, creating interoperability gaps and making security analysis per-device rather than per-spec.

spec_gaps.description: The ModeTags field is constrained to 1 to 8 tags per mode, but there is no normative rule preventing contradictory or hazardous tag combinations (e.g., Bake 0x4000 + Clean 0x4004, or Steam 0x4009 + Grill 0x4002). Tag interaction semantics are undefined.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Devices may expose logically contradictory or physically dangerous mode combinations. Formal verification of safety properties is impossible without deterministic tag combination semantics.

spec_gaps.description: The spec provides only illustrative examples of multi-tag modes (e.g., 'Convection Cook and Clean' with tags 0x4001 + 0x4004) without defining whether such combinations represent sequential operations, simultaneous activations, or implementation-specific blends. The example is informative, not normative.
spec_gaps.security_impact: Compound mode semantics are unpredictable across implementations. An attacker exploiting ChangeToMode to set a compound mode may trigger unexpected physical behavior that neither user nor safety system can predict.

spec_gaps.location: Section 8.11 (entire cluster — no timed interaction requirement)
spec_gaps.description: No command in the Oven Mode cluster requires a Timed Interaction (TimedInvoke = true). In clusters with physical safety impact (e.g., Door Lock requires timed interactions for unlock), the absence of timed invoke for hazardous mode changes (Clean, Grill) provides no replay attack protection.
spec_gaps.security_impact: A replayed or injected ChangeToMode command with a hazardous NewMode value (Clean, Grill) can activate heating elements without the time-bound freshness guarantee that timed interactions provide.

cluster_fsm.initial_state_note: Initial state is IMPLEMENTATION-DEFINED. StartUpMode attribute (0x0002) is disallowed (conformance X) and OnMode (0x0003) is disallowed (conformance X). The spec provides no normative initial value for CurrentMode. A conformant implementation must expose at least one mode with Bake tag (0x4000) in SupportedModes, but which mode is active at cluster initialization is unspecified.
cluster_invariants: EXISTS m IN SupportedModes WHERE (0x4000 IN m.ModeTags)
CurrentMode IN {m.Mode FOR m IN SupportedModes}
FORALL m IN SupportedModes: len(m.ModeTags) >= 1 && len(m.ModeTags) <= 8

fields.name: NewMode
fields.description: Mode value matching a Mode field in SupportedModes

inherited_from_mode_base.note: Commands and response types are defined in the Mode Base cluster (not re-specified in Section 8.11). This FSM models their behavior as applied to the Oven Mode derived cluster.
